subcategories.label: Time of day
subcategories.explanation: AM is 오전. PM is 오후. Hours use native; minutes & seconds use sino.
subcategories.example: 5:32:30 AM -> 오전 다섯 시 삼십이 분 삼십 초.

subcategories.label: Full date
subcategories.explanation: Dates use Sino numbers for year, month, and day. Days of the week are separate words, each ending in 요일.
subcategories.example: Sunday, May 3, 2026 -> 일요일 이천이십육 년 오월 삼 일.

subcategories.label: Beginning / middle / end of month
subcategories.explanation: 초 = beginning (early part), 중 = middle, 말 = end (late part) of a month. Attach directly after the month name.
subcategories.example: end of February → 이월 말. middle of October → 시월 중.

subcategories.label: Beginning / middle / end of year
subcategories.explanation: 초 = early part of the year, 중 = middle of the year, 말 = late part / end of the year. All attach after 년.
subcategories.example: beginning of 2025 → 이천이십오 년 초. end of 2024 → 이천이십사 년 말.

subcategories.label: Xth week of a month
subcategories.explanation: Use native ordinals 첫째/둘째/셋째/넷째/다섯째 for 1st–5th, and 마지막 for last. The pattern is [month] [ordinal] 주.
subcategories.example: the 2nd week of March → 삼월 둘째 주. the last week of October → 시월 마지막 주.

subcategories.label: Xth weekday of a month
subcategories.explanation: Use native ordinals 첫째/둘째/셋째/넷째/다섯째 for 1st–5th, and 마지막 for last. The pattern is [month] [ordinal] [요일].
subcategories.example: the 1st Monday of June → 유월 첫째 월요일. the last Friday of December → 십이월 마지막 금요일.

subcategories.label: Age
subcategories.explanation: Age has two forms - native numbers before 살, or sino numbers before 세.
subcategories.example: 24 years old -> 스물네 살 or 이십사 세.

subcategories.label: Birth year
subcategories.explanation: Birth years can either use sino or digit-style reading, usually expressed casually as the last two digits.
subcategories.example: born in 98 -> 구팔 년생.

categories.label: Won
categories.explanation: Korean won uses Sino numbers before 원.

subcategories.label: Korean phone number
subcategories.explanation: Korean phone numbers are read digit by digit, always starting with 010.
subcategories.example: 010-1234-5678 -> 공일공 일이삼사 오육칠팔.

subcategories.label: Western phone number
subcategories.explanation: Western phone numbers are read digit by digit.
subcategories.example: 212-555-0198 -> 이일이 오오오 공일구팔.

categories.label: Korean address
categories.explanation: Korean addresses put the road name first, then the building number. Apartment addresses also add 동 (building block) and 호 (unit number). All numbers use sino.

subcategories.label: Height
subcategories.explanation: Height units are transliterated: cm → 센티미터, ft → 피트, in → 인치. Numbers use sino.
subcategories.example: 175 cm → 백칠십오 센티미터.

subcategories.label: Weight
subcategories.explanation: Weight units are transliterated: kg → 킬로그램, g → 그램, lb → 파운드, oz → 온스. Numbers use sino.
subcategories.example: 65 kg → 육십오 킬로그램.

subcategories.label: Temperature
subcategories.explanation: Both °C and °F use 도. Negative temperatures use 영하 (below zero) before the number. Positive temperatures can say 영상 (above zero) or nothing — both are correct.
subcategories.example: -5°C → 영하 오 도. 25°C → 이십오 도 or 영상 이십오 도.

subcategories.label: Volume
subcategories.explanation: Volume units are transliterated: mL → 밀리리터, L → 리터, cups → 컵, tbsp → 큰술, tsp → 작은술. Numbers use sino.
subcategories.example: 350 mL → 삼백오십 밀리리터.

subcategories.label: Data volume
subcategories.explanation: Storage units are transliterated: KB → 킬로바이트, MB → 메가바이트, GB → 기가바이트, TB → 테라바이트. Numbers use sino.
subcategories.example: 32 GB → 삼십이 기가바이트.

subcategories.label: Distance / Length
subcategories.explanation: Distance units are transliterated: mm → 밀리미터, cm → 센티미터, m → 미터, km → 킬로미터, in → 인치, ft → 피트, mi → 마일. Numbers use sino.
subcategories.example: 5 km → 오 킬로미터.

subcategories.label: Area
subcategories.explanation: Square units use 제곱 before the transliterated unit: m² → 제곱미터, km² → 제곱킬로미터. Numbers use sino.
subcategories.example: 84 m² → 팔십사 제곱미터.

subcategories.label: Speed
subcategories.explanation: Speed uses 시속 (per hour) before km/h and mph, and 초속 (per second) before m/s. The number comes between the rate word and the unit.
subcategories.example: 60 km/h → 시속 육십 킬로미터.

subcategories.label: Data rate
subcategories.explanation: Data rate uses 초당 (per second) before the transliterated unit. Mbps → 메가비트, MB/s → 메가바이트, GB/s → 기가바이트. Numbers use sino.
subcategories.example: 100 Mbps → 초당 백 메가비트.

subcategories.label: Price per unit
subcategories.explanation: 당 means 'per' and attaches to the unit: 명당 (per person), 개당 (per item), 시간당 (per hour). For time units, 에 is also natural: 한 시간에, 하루에. Amount uses sino numbers + 원.
subcategories.example: ₩5,000 per person → 한 명당 오천 원.

subcategories.label: Frequency per time
subcategories.explanation: [Time period]에 [native count] 번 means '[count] times per [period]'. Time words: 하루 (1 day), 일주일 (1 week), 한 달 (1 month), 일 년 (1 year). Counts use native numbers.
subcategories.example: 3 times per week → 일주일에 세 번.

subcategories.label: Counters
subcategories.explanation: Native numbers come before the counter word. Common counters: 명 (people), 권 (books), 마리 (animals), 병 (bottles), 개 (general items), 장 (flat objects like paper/tickets), 대 (machines/vehicles), 잔 (cups/glasses).
subcategories.example: 5 people → 다섯 명. 3 books → 세 권. 2 cats → 두 마리.

subcategories.label: Numbered Labels
subcategories.explanation: Numbered labels use sino numbers. Common labels: 호선 (subway line), 번 출구 (exit), 편 (flight), 번 창구 (counter), 학년 (school grade), 반 (class), 점 (points/score), 주년 (anniversary), 세대 (generation).
subcategories.example: Subway Line 2 → 이 호선. Exit 5 → 오 번 출구. 5 points → 오 점. 1st anniversary → 일 주년. 3rd generation → 삼 세대.

subcategories.label: Fractions
subcategories.explanation: Fractions are said denominator-first: [denominator] 분의 [numerator]. Both use sino numbers. 분의 literally means 'parts of'.
subcategories.example: 3/4 → 사 분의 삼. 1/2 → 이 분의 일.

subcategories.label: Percents
subcategories.explanation: Percentages use 퍼센트 after the sino number. There is no special word for 'percent' — just say the number then 퍼센트.
subcategories.example: 75% → 칠십오 퍼센트. 12.5% → 십이 점 오 퍼센트.

subcategories.label: Ratios
subcategories.explanation: Ratios use 대 (literally 'against/versus') between two sino numbers. Pattern: [A] 대 [B]. Also used for sports scores.
subcategories.example: 3 : 1  → 삼 대 일. 2 : 5 → 이 대 오. Score of 3 to 0 → 삼 대 영.

subcategories.label: Times / 번
subcategories.explanation: 번 means 'time(s)' (as in occurrences). Native numbers are more common in speech, but sino numbers are also accepted.
subcategories.example: 3 times → 세 번 or 삼 번. 10 times → 열 번 or 십 번.

subcategories.label: Ordinal / 번째
subcategories.explanation: 번째 makes ordinal numbers. 1st is always 첫 번째 (special form). All other ordinals use native numbers + 번째.
subcategories.example: 1st → 첫 번째. 2nd → 두 번째. 5th → 다섯 번째.

subcategories.label: Ranking
subcategories.explanation: 등 means 'place' in a competition or ordering. Common for races, contests, class rank, and informal placements. Uses sino numbers. 위 means 'rank' on a chart, leaderboard, or formal ranking. Uses sino numbers. Often paired with 차트, 랭킹, or 순위. More formal than 등.
subcategories.example: 1st place → 일 등. 3rd place → 삼 등. 1st on the chart → 일 위. 5th in the rankings → 오 위

subcategories.label: Hours / minutes / seconds
subcategories.explanation: Hours use native numbers (한 시간, 두 시간, 세 시간…). Minutes and seconds use sino numbers (십오 분, 삼십 초…).
subcategories.example: 2 hours → 두 시간. 45 minutes → 사십오 분. 30 seconds → 삼십 초.

subcategories.label: Day durations
subcategories.explanation: Days 1–4 have special native Korean words: 하루 (1 day), 이틀 (2 days), 사흘 (3 days), 나흘 (4 days). From 5 onwards, sino numbers + 일 are used. The native words are still accepted alongside sino forms for 1–4.
subcategories.example: 1 day → 하루. 3 days → 사흘 or 삼 일. 7 days → 칠 일.

subcategories.label: Weeks / months / years
subcategories.explanation: Weeks and years use sino numbers: 주 (weeks), 년 (years). Months accept native + 달 (한 달, 두 달…) or sino + 개월 (삼 개월, 육 개월…). 개월 is typical for durations; 달 is common in everyday speech.
subcategories.example: 3 weeks → 삼 주. 6 months → 여섯 달 or 육 개월. 2 years → 이 년.

subcategories.label: Floors
subcategories.explanation: 층 means 'floor'. Above-ground floors: sino number + 층. Underground floors: 지하 + sino number + 층.
subcategories.example: 3F → 삼 층. B2 → 지하 이 층.

subcategories.label: Room numbers
subcategories.explanation: 호 means 'room' or 'unit number'. Uses standard sino whole-number reading for typical room/apartment numbers.
subcategories.example: Room 305 → 삼백오 호. Unit 1402 → 천사백이 호.

categories.label: Hotel stays / nights and days
categories.explanation: Hotel stays use X 박 Y 일 (X nights, Y days). 박 = nights, 일 = days. The number of days is always nights + 1. Both use sino numbers.
categories.example: 3 nights, 4 days → 삼 박 사 일. 1 night, 2 days → 일 박 이 일.

subcategories.label: Addition
subcategories.explanation: 더하기 means 'plus'. Structure: [A] 더하기 [B]는/은 [C]. 는 follows vowel-ending syllables; 은 follows consonant-ending syllables — the topic particle attaches to B.
subcategories.example: 3 + 5 = 8 → 삼 더하기 오는 팔.

subcategories.label: Subtraction
subcategories.explanation: 빼기 means 'minus'. Structure: [A] 빼기 [B]는/은 [C]. The topic particle 는/은 attaches to B based on whether B ends in a vowel or consonant.
subcategories.example: 10 - 3 = 7 → 십 빼기 삼은 칠.

subcategories.label: Multiplication
subcategories.explanation: 곱하기 means 'times / multiplied by'. Structure: [A] 곱하기 [B]는/은 [C]. The topic particle 는/은 attaches to B.
subcategories.example: 4 × 7 = 28 → 사 곱하기 칠은 이십팔.

subcategories.label: Division
subcategories.explanation: 나누기 means 'divided by'. Structure: [A] 나누기 [B]는/은 [C]. The topic particle 는/은 attaches to the divisor B.
subcategories.example: 12 ÷ 3 = 4 → 십이 나누기 삼은 사.

subcategories.label: More than / at least
subcategories.explanation: 이상 means at least (≥). 초과 means strictly more than (>). Both use sino numbers.
subcategories.example: at least 5 → 오 이상. more than 10 (strict) → 십 초과.

subcategories.label: Less than / at most
subcategories.explanation: 이하 means at most (≤). 미만 means strictly less than (<). Both use sino numbers.
subcategories.example: at most 20 → 이십 이하. less than 10 (strict) → 십 미만.

subcategories.label: Between
subcategories.explanation: 사이 means between. All numbers use sino. For the particle form, attach 와 to a vowel-final word and 과 to a consonant-final word.
subcategories.example: between 3 and 7 → 삼과 칠 사이. between 60 and 65 → 육십과 육십오 사이.

categories.label: Time particles

templates.question: within {n} {unit.en} (i.e. i must do it within {n} {unit.en})

categories.explanation: Time-relation particles: 뒤/후 (after/later), 전 (ago), 안에 (within), 만에 (accomplished in a duration). Days 1–4 use native words (하루, 이틀, 사흘, 나흘) before the particle; sino + 일 is also accepted. Months accept native + 달 or sino + 개월. Hours use native numbers; minutes/seconds use sino.
categories.example: 3 hours later → 세 시간 뒤. 2 days ago → 이틀 전 or 이 일 전. 3 months later → 세 달 뒤 or 삼 개월 뒤.

subcategories.label: Age decades
subcategories.explanation: Age decades use 20대/30대 etc. Early/mid/late map to 초반/중반/후반 respectively.
subcategories.example: late 20s -> 20대 후반

subcategories.label: Year decades
subcategories.explanation: Year decades use 1990년대 style. Early/mid/late refer to parts of the decade.
subcategories.example: early 2000s -> 2000년대 초반
